creator: rfg@tristatelogic.com
text:
In message <20130428214916.GJ69834@birne.dunkelkammer.void>, you wrote:

>Hello Ronald,
>
>unfortunately, there hasn't been any activity on your problem report on
>print/ghostview (see [1]) for quite a while.

Yes.  Apparently not.

>What is the state of this
>issue? Are you still seeing the problem you described,

I don't know.  I have not had occasion to try again to render any .PS
documents since the time that I filed that PR.  And due to circumstances
beyind my control, I have not had time to do any updating to my various
installed ports since around that time either, so if I were to just try
again now, the reslts would probably not be informative, relative to what-
ever is in the _current_ official ports tree.

>or should this PR be closed?

I don't know what the policies of the FreeBSD developers are generally,
but if you were to ask me for my personal opinion, I personally would
not want to close open PRs unless and until some developer had at least
tried to see if the problem is readily reproducable.  Has anyone other
than me even tried to see if the problem is readily reproducable?

>If the problem still exists: Is it really FreeBSD-specific, or
>is ghostview crashing on other systems (e.g. Linux), too?

I really would not know the answer to that.  I am a FreeBSD bigot.  I do
not use Linux at the present time.
